Name: Paras
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Burrows under the ground to gnaw on tree roots. The mushrooms on its back absorb most of the nutrition.

Name: Parasect
Effects: Choose 3 of your Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a different type of basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The larger the mushroom on its back grows, the stronger the mushroom spores it scatters.

Name: Pinsir
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This attack does 20 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It swings its long pincer horns wildly to attack. During cold periods, it hides deep in forests.

Name: Cacnea
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It prefers harsh environments such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.

Name: Pansage
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It’s good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It’s kind enough to share them with friends.

Name: Simisage
Effects: Put 3 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
This attack does 10 damage times the number of cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

Name: Chespin
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Such a thick shell of wood covers its head and back that even a direct hit from a truck wouldn’t faze it.

Name: Chespin
Flavor-Text: Such a thick shell of wood covers its head and back that even a direct hit from a truck wouldn’t faze it.

Name: Chespin
Effects: Search your deck for a [G] Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

Name: Quilladin
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: They strengthen their lower bodies by running into one another. They are very kind and won’t start fights.

Name: Chesnaught
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: When it takes a defensive posture with its fists guarding its face, it could withstand a bomb blast.

Name: Chesnaught BREAK
Effects: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself. This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
BREAK-Rule: Chesnaught BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Scatterbug
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

Name: Spewpa
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: The beaks of birds can’t begin to scratch its stalwart body. To defend itself, it spews powder.

Name: Vivillon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The patterns on this Pokémon’s wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales.

Name: Skiddo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If it has sunshine and water, it doesn’t need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back.

Name: Gogoat
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They inhabit mountainous regions. The leader of the herd is decided by a battle of clashing horns.

Name: Cyndaquil
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a timid nature. If it is startled, the flames on its back burn more vigorously.

Name: Quilava
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is an Energy card, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Be careful if it turns its back during battle. It means that it will attack with the fire on its back.

Name: Typhlosion
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 80 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. Then, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur together to cause huge explosions.

Name: Houndoom-EX
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Houndoom-EX
Effects: You may discard all [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pansear
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Simisear
Effects: Put 3 [W] Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
This attack does 10 damage times the number of cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: When it gets excited, embers rise from its head and tail and it gets hot. For some reason, it loves sweets.

Name: Fennekin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

Name: Braixen
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies.

Name: Goldeen
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its dorsal, pectoral and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the water dancer.

Name: Seaking
Effects: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest by hollowing out boulders in streams with its horn. It defends its eggs with its life.

Name: Staryu
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.

Name: Starmie
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 7 cards.
If you and your opponent have the same number of Benched Pokémon, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.

Name: Remoraid
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a Mantine’s underside to scavenge for leftovers.

Name: Remoraid
Effects: Discard any Stadium card in play.
Flavor-Text: Using its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a Mantine’s underside to scavenge for leftovers.

Name: Octillery
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It traps foes with the suction cups on its tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

Name: Glalie-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 100 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Glalie-EX
Effects: If this Pokémon has 10 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Piplup
Flavor-Text: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

Name: Prinplup
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees.

Name: Empoleon
Ability-Effect: Each of your Basic Pokémon’s attacks does 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.

Name: Snover
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: In the spring, it grows berries with the texture of frozen treats around its belly.

Name: Abomasnow
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [N] Pokémon, it is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

Name: Panpour
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

Name: Simipour
Effects: Put 3 [G] Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
This attack does 10 damage times the number of cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: It prefers places with clean water. When its tuft runs low, it replenishes it by siphoning up water with its tail.

Name: Vanillite
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. It sleeps buried in snow.

Name: Vanillish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

Name: Vanilluxe
Effects: Until the end of your next turn, each player can’t play any Supporter or Stadium cards from his or her hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards.

Name: Froakie
Flavor-Text: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

Name: Frogadier
Flavor-Text: Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute’s time.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has small electric sacs on both its cheeks. If threatened, it looses electric charges from the sacs.

Name: Raichu
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon-EX. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It can loose 100,000-volt bursts of electricity instantly downing foes several times its size.

Name: Raichu BREAK
Effects: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.
BREAK-Rule: Raichu BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Magnemite
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, its Retreat Cost is [C] less for each Magnemite on your Bench.
Flavor-Text: It moves while constantly hovering. It discharges Thunder Wave and so on from the units at its sides.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It moves while constantly hovering. It discharges Thunder Wave and so on from the units at its sides.

Name: Magneton
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Generates strange radio signals. It raises the temperature by 3.6 degrees Fahrenheit within 3,300 feet.

Name: Magnezone
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [L] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: Discard a [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It evolved from exposure to a special magnetic field. Three units generate magnetism.

Name: Raikou
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [L] Energy attached to it, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This rough Pokémon stores energy inside its body, then sweeps across the land, shooting off electricity.

Name: Stunfisk
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers aa jolt of electricity.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It uses its tail to absorb electricity from power plants or from outlets in houses, and then it fires the electricity from its whiskers.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

Name: Haunter
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may leave both Active Pokémon Confused.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. That Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It licks with its gaseous tongue to steal the victim’s life force. It lurks in darkness for prey.

Name: Gengar
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has 3 or more damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: Hiding in people’s shadows at night, it absorbs their heat. The chill it causes makes the victims shake.

Name: Mewtwo-EX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mewtwo-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of [P] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Switch all damage counters on this Pokémon with those on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Mewtwo-EX
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 50 more damage, and this attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance or any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Mewtwo-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Misdreavus
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It likes playing mischievous tricks, such as screaming and wailing to startle people at night.

Name: Mismagius
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may have your opponent shuffle his or her hand into his or her deck and draw a card for each of his or her remaining Prize cards.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its cries sound like incantations to torment the foe. It appears where you least expect it.

Name: Wobbuffet
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

Name: Ralts
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

Name: Cresselia
Ability-Effect: If there is any Stadium card is in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Those who sleep holding Cresselia’s feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

Name: Woobat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put a card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: The heart-shaped mark left on a body after a woobat has been attached to it is said to bring good fortune.

Name: Swoobat
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Returning Echo attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If tails, return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.
Flavor-Text: Anyone who comes into contact with the ultrasonic waves emitted by a courting male experiences a positive mood shift.

Name: Elgyem
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Psych Up attack does 20 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Rumors of its origin are linked to a UFO crash site in the desert 50 years ago.

Name: Beheeyem
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon times the amount of Energy attached to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It uses psychic power to control an opponent’s brain and tamper with its memories.

Name: Sandshrew
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Disliking water, it lives in deep burrows in arid areas. It can roll itself instantly into a ball.

Name: Sandslash
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Curls up into a spiny ball when threatened. It can roll while curled up to attack or escape.

Name: Cubone
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It always wears the skull of its dead mother, so no one has any idea what its hidden face looks like.

Name: Marowak
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The bone it holds is its key weapon. It throws the bone skillfully like a boomerang to KO targets.

Name: Marowak BREAK
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
BREAK-Rule: Marowak BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Swinub
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: If it smells something enticing, it dashes off headlong to find the source of the aroma.

Name: Piloswine
Effects: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 10 more damage for each [C] in the Retreat Cost of your Swinub, Piloswine, and Mamoswine.
Flavor-Text: Because the long hair all over its body obscures its sight, it just keeps charging repeatedly.

Name: Mamoswine
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s [R] or [W] Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If you have a Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 more damage. If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its impressive tusks are made of ice. The population thinned when it turned warm after the ice age.

Name: Hippopotas
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It enshrouds itself with sand to protect itself from germs. It does not enjoy getting wet.

Name: Gallade
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.
Effects: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A master of courtesy and swordsmanship, it fights using extending swords on its elbows.

Name: Meloetta
Effects: Attach 2 [F] Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon. Then, switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If this Pokémon has any [P] Energy attached to it, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

Name: Pancham
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can’t help grinning if anyone pats its head.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against.

Name: Cacturne
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Packs of them follow travelers through the desert until the travelers can no longer move.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

Name: Zoroark
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

Name: Zoroark BREAK
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
BREAK-Rule: Zoroark BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Inkay
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It flashes the light-emitting spots on its body, which drains its opponent’s will to fight. It takes the opportunity to scuttle away and hide.

Name: Yveltal
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, each Pokémon Tool card in play has no effect.
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon-EX. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Ancient people believed that petitioning Bronzong for rain was the way to make crops grow.

Name: Mr. Mime
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by attacks.
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is adept at conning people. It is said to be able to create walls out of thin air by miming.

Name: Snubbull
Effects: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Small Pokémon flee from its scary face. It is, however, considered by women to be cute.

Name: Granbull
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 3 random cards from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into his or her deck.
This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It can make most any Pokémon run away simply by opening its mouth wide to reveal its big fangs.

Name: Ralts
Flavor-Text: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

Name: Flabébé
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: When it finds a flower it likes, it dwells on that flower its whole life long. It floats in the wind’s embrace with an untroubled heart.

Name: Floette
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the flowers of a well-tended flower bed bloom, it appears and celebrates with an elegant dance.

Name: Florges
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon’s attacks costs [Y] less.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: In times long past, governors of castles would invite Florges to create flower gardens to embellish the castle domains.

Name: Florges BREAK
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 30 damage and remove a Special Condition from your Active Pokémon.
BREAK-Rule: Florges BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Spritzee
Effects: This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In the past, rather than using perfume, royal ladies carried a Spritzee that would waft a fragrance they liked.

Name: Aromatisse
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. Put 6 damage counters instead of 3 on that Pokémon for this Special Condition.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its scent is so overpowering that, unless a Trainer happens to really enjoy the smell, he or she will have a hard time walking alongside it.

Name: Xerneas
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.
If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When the horns on its head shine in seven different colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.

Name: Axew
Flavor-Text: They mark their territory by leaving gashes in trees with their tusks. If a tusk breaks, a new one grows in quickly.

Name: Axew
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They mark their territory by leaving gashes in trees with their tusks. If a tusk breaks, a new one grows in quickly.

Name: Fraxure
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [N] Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: A broken tusk will not grow back, so it diligently sharpens its tusks on river rocks after the end of a battle.

Name: Haxorus
Effects: As long as this Haxorus is your Active Pokémon, each of its attacks does 100 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance). You can’t add more than 100 damage in this way.
Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Their sturdy tusks will stay sharp even if used to cut steel beams. These Pokémon are covered in hard armor.

Name: Noivern
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent’s hand.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The ultrasonic waves it emits from its ears can reduce a large boulder to pebbles. It emerges out of the dark to attack.

Name: Noivern BREAK
Effects: If you have the same amount of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.
BREAK-Rule: Noivern BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

Name: Doduo
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.
Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

Name: Doduo
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

Name: Dodrio
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C][C] less.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: An odd species that is rarely found. The three heads respectively represent joy, sadness, and anger.

Name: Snorlax
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

Name: Hoothoot
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Item cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.
Flavor-Text: It always stand on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

Name: Noctowl
Effects: Each player reveals his or her hand. This attack does 20 damage times the number of Item cards revealed.
Flavor-Text: When it needs to think, it rotates its head 180 degrees to sharpen its intellectual power.

Name: Teddiursa
Flavor-Text: Before food becomes scarce in wintertime, its habit is to hoard food in many hidden locations.

Name: Ursaring
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Although it has a large body, it is quite skilled at climbing trees. It eats and sleeps in the treetops.

Name: Smeargle
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch a basic Energy attached to your Active Pokémon with a different type of basic Energy card from your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: Once it becomes an adult, it has a tendency to let its comrades plant footprints on its back.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

Name: Starly
Effects: Search your deck for as many Starly as you like and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

Name: Staravia
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in forests and fields. Squabbles over territory occur when flocks collide.

Name: Staraptor
Effects: Before doing damage, have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

Name: Rufflet
Flavor-Text: They will challenge anything, even strong opponents, without fear. Their frequent fights help them become stronger.

Name: Braviary
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.

Name: Noibat
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Even a robust wrestler will become dizzy and unable to stand when exposed to its 200,000-hertz ultrasonic waves.

Name: Noibat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Even a robust wrestler will become dizzy and unable to stand when exposed to its 200,000-hertz ultrasonic waves.

Name: Assault Vest
Description: Any damage done to the Pokémon this card is attached to by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon that have any Special Energy attached to them is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Brigette
Description: Search your deck for 1 Basic Pokémon-EX or 3 Basic Pokémon (except for Pokémon-EX) and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Buddy-Buddy Rescue
Description: Each player puts a Pokémon from his or her discard pile into his or her hand. (Your opponent chooses first.)
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Fisherman
Description: Put 4 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Float Stone
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Parallel City
Description: Choose which way this card faces before you play it. This ↓ player can’t have more than 3 Benched Pokémon. (When this card comes into play, this ↓ player discards Benched Pokémon until he or she has 3 Pokémon on the Bench.)Choose which way this card faces before you play it. Any damage done by attacks from this ↓ player’s [G], [R], or [W] Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Professor’s Letter
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Skyla
Description: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rainbow Energy
Description: This card provides [C] Energy. While in play, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon.

Attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
